title: Filter Logs With grep
description:
## Count the Errors

A sample access log has been placed at **`/root/access.log`**. Each line ends with an HTTP status code, for example:

```
10.0.0.2 GET /api 500
```

**Your task:** count how many lines have the status **`500`**, and write **just that number** to **`/root/result.txt`**.

> 💡 `grep -c` counts matching lines. Redirect the count into the result file.

hints.title: Count matching lines
hints.body: grep -c prints the number of lines that match a pattern. Redirect it to the result file.

hints.title: Check your answer
hints.body: Print the file to confirm it holds a single number.

setup_commands.command:
printf '10.0.0.1 GET /home 200
10.0.0.2 GET /api 500
10.0.0.3 POST /login 200
10.0.0.4 GET /api 500
10.0.0.5 GET /x 404
' > /root/access.log

validation.description: Checks that /root/result.txt contains the count of status-500 lines (2).
